title: CC and BCC
drDigitalIntro: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of an email to someone extra — everyone can see who was CC'd. BCC (Blind Carbon Copy) is a secret copy — the main recipient can't see who was BCC'd. Use CC to keep people informed, like copying a coworker on a work email. Use BCC when you want someone to see an email privately, or when emailing a large group where people don't know each other.

playgroundTask.goal: You used CC and BCC to send copies of an email

steps.say: Click Compose to start a new email.
Type 'mom@example.com' in the To field, then click another field.
Type 'sister@example.com' in the CC field (everyone will see this).
Type 'me@example.com' in the BCC field (secret copy for yourself).

drDigitalSuccess: CC = visible copy, BCC = secret copy. Now you can share emails with the right people!
drDigitalHint: Fill in each field one at a time and click the next field (or press Tab) to move on.
